title: Terminal Mental Models, Help, and Evidence · Terminal Checkpoint
summary: Investigate an unfamiliar Linux workstation without guessing, changing host state, or treating copied commands as explanations. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a terminal orientation and command-evidence notebook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish terminal, shell, command, process, kernel, and operating-system responsibilities in an observed interaction.
Parse command names, options, operands, quoting, and exit status before predicting behavior.
Use man pages, help, type, command -V, info, and project documentation to establish command authority and provenance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Shell Terminal Kernel · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — On a quiz host, learner starts in /home/learner/lab with Bash 5.3, Coreutils 9.11, and exit status 0. Resolve ./data/../scripts, identify command authority, and separate terminal, shell, process, and kernel evidence. Competency probe: Distinguish terminal, shell, command, process, kernel, and operating-system responsibilities in an observed interaction.

Which action demonstrates Shell Terminal Kernel without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish terminal, shell, command, process, kernel, and operating-system responsibilities in an observed interaction.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Terminal Evidence Record: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — On a quiz host, learner starts in /home/learner/lab with Bash 5.3, Coreutils 9.11, and exit status 1. Resolve ./data/../scripts, identify command authority, and separate terminal, shell, process, and kernel evidence. Competency probe: Record command, expected signal, actual output, exit status, interpretation, and next decision as separate evidence.
steps.why: Retrieving Terminal Evidence Record through the concrete a terminal orientation and command-evidence notebook case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record command, expected signal, actual output, exit status, interpretation, and next decision as separate evidence.
